message:
test(evidence): add comprehensive franken_evidence integration tests (bd-qaaxt.4)

14 integration tests covering: proptest random validation (500 cases),
serde roundtrip with float tolerance, concurrent writer safety (10
threads), full builder-to-JSONL-to-render pipeline, schema version
migration/graceful handling, rendering determinism (100 runs), JSONL
rotation, and edge cases. Adds proptest dev-dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.3","title":"[TESTING] Implement fault injection for crash/restart","description":"Implement fault injection for simulating actor crash and restart in Asupersync. Crash faults test whether the supervision tree correctly detects failed actors, cleans up their obligations, and restarts them. Implementation: (1) Crash injection: kill actor at random point during execution (after N operations or at specific protocol step), (2) Restart model: restart actor with fresh state (cold restart) or from last checkpoint (warm restart), (3) Cascade crash: crash supervisor to test supervision tree recovery. Lab runtime integration: crashes are scheduled events with deterministic timing. Pass criteria: (a) All obligations held by crashed actor are released (no leak), (b) Supervisor detects crash and restarts within configured timeout, (c) Restarted actor resumes from correct protocol state, (d) In-flight messages to crashed actor are properly handled (timeout or redirect), (e) Crash during two-phase commit triggers abort, not partial commit. Must test: single crash, double crash, supervisor crash, crash during cancellation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:52.775914435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:08:48.177327675Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:08:35.086681046Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","fault-injection","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:23Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1408,"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verified and fixed crash/restart fault injection. Fixes: clippy finish_non_exhaustive on CrashController Debug impl, integration test deadlock (100 sends into capacity-16 channel), redundant clone. 16 unit + 21 integration tests all passing, clippy clean, fmt clean. Commit 75c3895.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:51:26Z"},{"id":1410,"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented CrashController + CrashSender<T> in src/channel/crash.rs. Supports probabilistic and deterministic crash triggers, cold/warm restart modes, restart exhaustion limits. 37 tests (16 unit + 21 integration) covering all 10 pass criteria. Committed as 75c3895.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:08:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.4","title":"[TESTING] Implement fault injection for clock skew","description":"Implement fault injection for simulating clock skew between Asupersync actors. Clock skew tests whether timeout-based protocols (lease expiry, budget deadlines, heartbeat detection) remain correct when clocks diverge.

BACKGROUND: In distributed systems, clock skew is a fundamental source of bugs. Even with NTP, clocks can diverge by milliseconds (normal) to seconds (pathological). The CALM theorem (Graveyard section 13.3) notes that monotone programs are clock-insensitive, but threshold-based operations (lease expiry, timeout detection) are inherently clock-dependent. Testing with simulated clock skew validates that the system uses causal ordering (logical clocks) rather than wall-clock assumptions for correctness-critical decisions.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define ClockSkewInjector struct with per_actor_offset: HashMap<ActorId, Duration> for static skew and drift_rate: HashMap<ActorId, f64> for progressive drift (ms/s). (2) Hook into the virtual time system from bd-1hu19.3: each actors VirtualClock applies its offset before returning the current time. (3) Clock jump: schedule a sudden offset change at a specific virtual time point to simulate NTP correction. (4) Monotonicity violation: temporarily return a time earlier than the previous read (detects code that assumes monotonic clocks). (5) Integration with FrankenLab scenario YAML: clock_skew section in fault schedule specifying per-actor offsets, drift rates, and jump events. (6) Use quanta crate for high-resolution clock abstraction that can be swapped for virtual clock in tests.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Lease expiry is correct despite clock skew -- use causal ordering, not wall-clock, for lease validity determination. Specifically: a lease acquired at logical time T with duration D expires at logical time T+D regardless of wall-clock skew. (2) Budget deadlines account for worst-case skew -- budget remaining calculation uses the maximum clock reading across all participants. (3) Heartbeat detection uses logical clocks -- a heartbeat received with a different wall-clock timestamp is accepted if the logical clock is within bounds. (4) No liveness violation -- system makes progress despite bounded clock skew (verified: all sagas complete within 2x the non-skewed baseline time). (5) Clock jump does not cause spurious lease expiry or budget exhaustion. (6) Monotonicity violation is detected and logged as a warning (system continues, does not crash).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each clock skew injection emits structured log: actor_id, original_time_ms, skewed_time_ms, offset_ms, drift_rate, event_type (static_offset|drift|jump|monotonicity_violation). All injected faults logged to EvidenceLedger: component=clock_skew_injector, action=skew_applied, top_features includes max_skew_ms, affected_actor_count. Aggregate stats at test completion: max_observed_skew_ms, monotonicity_violations_count, lease_expiry_correctness (pass/fail count).

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Unit tests: verify ClockSkewInjector correctly applies offsets and drift rates. (2) Lease tests: acquire lease under various skew conditions (0ms, 10ms, 100ms, 1s), verify correct expiry. (3) Budget tests: propagate budget through actors with different clock offsets, verify deadline correctness. (4) Heartbeat tests: verify heartbeat detection with skewed clocks. (5) Property tests (proptest): for any bounded clock skew (< 1s), all protocols eventually complete. (6) Integration: run full saga under clock skew via FrankenLab scenario, verify identical outcome to non-skewed execution.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:59.965023589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:34.683055179Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:22.798011739Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clock-skew","fault-injection","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1409,"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented clock skew fault injection. SkewClock wraps TimeSource with static offset, drift, one-time jump, and jitter. Integrates with TimerDriver. 18 unit + 15 integration tests, clippy clean, fmt clean. Commit 59eec7f.","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:07:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.5","title":"[TESTING] Loom-based concurrency tests for scheduler queues","description":"Implement Loom-based concurrency tests for Asupersync's scheduler queues and lock-free data structures. Loom exhaustively explores all possible thread interleavings to find concurrency bugs that are nearly impossible to catch with random testing.

BACKGROUND (Tier C Formal Assurance, Graveyard section 0.14): Loom provides model-checked concurrency testing by controlling thread scheduling and exploring all valid interleavings. Unlike stress tests that probabilistically find bugs, loom guarantees that if a bug exists within the exploration bound, it will be found. This corresponds to Tier C (Deductive Verification) on the formal assurance ladder because it provides exhaustive coverage within bounds.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Add loom as a dev-dependency behind a cfg(loom) feature flag. (2) Replace std::sync::atomic with loom::sync::atomic in testable code using conditional imports: #[cfg(loom)] use loom::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize; #[cfg(not(loom))] use std::sync::atomic::AtomicUsize. (3) Create a separate test binary: tests/loom_tests.rs that only runs under cargo test --features loom. (4) Use loom::model(|| { ... }) to wrap each test case. (5) For work-stealing deque: test push/pop from owner thread, steal from multiple thief threads, verify no lost items and no duplicates. (6) For MPSC channel: test concurrent sends from N producers, single consumer recv, verify total ordering within each producer and no message loss. (7) For task state machine: test concurrent wake/cancel/complete transitions, verify exactly one terminal state is reached. (8) For budget counter: test concurrent decrements from N threads, verify counter never goes below zero and final value equals initial minus total decrements.

COVERAGE TARGETS: (1) Work-stealing deque: all push/pop/steal interleavings with 2 threads, 3 operations each. (2) MPSC channel: all send/recv interleavings with 3 senders, 1 receiver, 2 messages each. (3) Task state machine: all wake/cancel/complete interleavings with 2 threads. (4) Budget counter: all decrement/check interleavings with 3 threads, 2 decrements each.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) All loom tests pass with max_preemptions=2 (CI fast mode). (2) All loom tests pass with max_preemptions=4 (nightly thorough mode). (3) No data races detected by loom (loom catches these automatically via its memory model). (4) No deadlocks (all model executions terminate within bounded steps). (5) Correct final state in all explored interleavings. (6) Loom tests complete in < 5 minutes for CI, < 30 minutes for nightly.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each loom test logs: test_name, max_preemptions, total_interleavings_explored, execution_time_ms, pass_fail. On failure: log the specific interleaving that caused the failure (loom provides a trace of scheduling decisions). Summary at test suite completion: total_tests, total_interleavings, unique_bugs_found. All output in structured JSON for CI parsing.

TESTING APPROACH (meta): (1) Start with the simplest data structure (budget counter) to validate the loom test infrastructure. (2) Progressively add more complex structures. (3) Use loom::thread::yield_now() at critical points to increase exploration coverage. (4) Compare loom results against tokio-test stress tests to validate that loom catches bugs stress tests miss.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:09.545514942Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:40:48.342030150Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T22:40:48.341895630Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","loom","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:15Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2ktrc.6","title":"[TESTING] E2E test: deterministic replay parity across 1000 random schedules","description":"End-to-end test that validates Asupersync's deterministic replay guarantee across 1000 random schedules using the Lab runtime.

BACKGROUND: Deterministic replay is Asupersync's core value proposition -- the ability to record a concurrent execution and replay it identically on any machine. This test validates that promise at scale. The FrankenLab deterministic scheduler (bd-1hu19.2) controls all nondeterministic choices (thread scheduling, timer resolution, I/O ordering) and records them to a trace file. Replay uses the same trace to make identical choices.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Use FrankenLab's deterministic scheduler with random seed generation: let seed = rand::thread_rng().gen::<u64>(); (2) Test program: a realistic workload with multiple concurrent tasks, channels, obligations, cancellation, and budget propagation. At minimum: 10 tasks, 5 channels, 3 obligation types, 1 cancellation event, budget propagation through 3 levels. (3) For each of 1000 iterations: (a) Generate random seed, (b) Run workload under Lab scheduler with recording, (c) Capture output hash (SHA-256 of all observable side effects: task return values, channel message order, obligation lifecycle events), (d) Replay from recorded trace, (e) Capture replay output hash, (f) Assert record_hash == replay_hash. (4) Use rayon::par_iter for parallel execution of the 1000 iterations. (5) Store all seeds and hashes in a results CSV for analysis.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) 100% replay determinism: all 1000 iterations produce identical record/replay hashes. (2) Test completes in < 10 minutes on CI (parallelized). (3) Any replay divergence produces a detailed diff showing the first diverging event with full context. (4) Test is itself deterministic: running the test with the same meta-seed produces the same 1000 sub-seeds. (5) Cross-platform: hashes are identical on x86_64 and aarch64 (floating-point determinism verified).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Per-iteration structured JSON log: iteration_index, seed, record_hash, replay_hash, match (bool), record_time_ms, replay_time_ms, event_count. Summary log at completion: total_iterations, match_count, mismatch_count, determinism_rate (target 1.0), total_wall_time_ms, avg_replay_overhead_percent. On divergence: full event-level diff with timestamps, task IDs, and event types for both record and replay traces. All logs emitted to both stdout (CI visible) and JSONL file for offline analysis.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Smoke test: single iteration with known seed, verify manually computed expected hash. (2) Regression: if any iteration ever fails, add its seed to a pinned regression set that runs on every CI build. (3) Statistical: track determinism rate over time in a dashboard -- any rate below 1.0 is a P0 bug.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:16.147531077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:36:44.519091750Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic-replay","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Added: (1) seed-sweep harness test deterministic_replay_parity_seed_sweep_1000, (2) richer parity workload with mixed tasks + cancellation, (3) deterministic meta-seed/iteration env controls, (4) structured JSON/CSV artifact emission (target/replay in CI or env override), (5) SHA-256 record/replay hash comparison + mismatch diagnostics.\n\nValidation run:\n- cargo fmt --check ✅\n- cargo check --all-targets ✅ (shared-tree warning in src/lab/scenario_runner.rs)\n- cargo test --test replay_e2e_suite deterministic_replay_parity_seed_sweep_1000 -- --nocapture ✅\n  result: 1000/1000 matched, determinism_ppm=1_000_000, total_wall_time_ms≈4400.\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings ❌ blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy violations in src/lab/scenario_runner.rs and src/obligation/saga.rs in current shared tree.\n\nKeeping bead in_progress pending broader acceptance hardening (cross-platform hash parity, additional workload dimensions, CI profile integration).","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:50:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ktrc.7","title":"[TESTING] E2E test: no obligation leaks under 10M random schedules","description":"End-to-end stress test that runs 10 million random schedules with the Lab runtime and verifies zero obligation leaks. For each schedule: (1) Spawn a random DAG of tasks with random obligations (SendPermit, Lease, Reserve), (2) Apply random faults (cancel, timeout, panic), (3) After quiescence, verify all obligations have been released (obligation counter == 0), (4) Log any leaks with full schedule trace for reproduction. Configuration: Use rayon for parallel execution across cores. Target: complete 10M schedules in <30 minutes on CI. Statistics: Track leak rate with exact binomial confidence interval — target is 0 leaks with 95% CI upper bound <1e-7. This is the ultimate obligation safety validation — if we can survive 10M random schedules without a leak, the system is robust.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:25.150268482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:23:26.303864660Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","obligation-leaks","stress","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:29Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ktrc.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2ktrc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:29Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kwn","title":"Implement ShutdownStats collection for server drain lifecycle","description":"ShutdownStats is defined in src/server/shutdown.rs (drained, force_closed, duration) but never populated. Implement:
1. Track drain start time when begin_drain() is called
2. Add a drain_with_stats() method that orchestrates the full drain flow: wait for connections to close, handle timeout, count drained vs force-closed, produce ShutdownStats
3. Add tests for stats collection","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMountain","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:22:24.936879277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T17:40:39.196070728Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T17:40:39.195985760Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2l6g","title":"E2E: Cancellation Protocol Stress Tests","description":"Dedicated E2E stress tests for cancellation protocol. Scenarios: cancel during I/O ops (TCP, DNS, file), cancel during lock acquisition, concurrent cancellations, cancel with pending obligations (commit-or-abort), deep cancel propagation, cancel with timers, loser drain after races with real I/O. Log state machine transitions (request > drain > finalize) with timestamps.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:13:53.062550650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:05:23.327394377Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T19:05:23.327328364Z","close_reason":"Added 7 cancellation stress tests: pending obligations, deep propagation (10 levels), concurrent cancel reasons (6 kinds), timer interleave, race loser drain, obligation commit interruption, deterministic cancel storm. All pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2l6g","depends_on_id":"bd-1dgr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2l6g","depends_on_id":"bd-21f9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1315,"issue_id":"bd-2l6g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Critical for asupersync's core value proposition. The cancellation protocol (request > drain > finalize) is the distinguishing feature. Must stress-test: cancel during real I/O, cancel with obligations, deep propagation, loser drain after races. Log every state machine transition.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:15:10Z"},{"id":1316,"issue_id":"bd-2l6g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every test function must: (1) call init_test_logging() at start; (2) use test_phase!("test_name") for the top-level test marker; (3) use test_section!("phase") for each distinct phase (setup, execution, verification, teardown); (4) use assert_with_log!() instead of bare assert!() for all assertions that compare values; (5) call test_complete!("test_name") on success; (6) log key intermediate state (e.g., connection count, region tree depth, bytes transferred) at INFO level with tracing::info!() so failures can be diagnosed from log output alone.","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:29:50Z"}]}
@@ -2962,8 +2962,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-qaaxt.1","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement EvidenceLedger struct with serde serialization","description":"Implement the core EvidenceLedger struct in the franken_evidence crate with full serde serialization support. Fields: ts_unix_ms: u64, component: String, action: String, posterior: Vec<f64>, expected_loss_by_action: HashMap<String, f64>, chosen_expected_loss: f64, calibration_score: f64, fallback_active: bool, top_features: Vec<(String, f64)>. Must implement: Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug. Serialization format must be compact (use short field names in serde rename) for JSONL efficiency. Include a builder pattern for ergonomic construction. Include validation: posterior must sum to ~1.0, calibration_score in [0,1], expected losses non-negative. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: evidence_ledger::emit with component, action, trace_id. DEBUG: EvidenceLedger entry constructed: component={component}, action={action}, posterior_len={n}, calibration={cal:.3f}. WARN: EvidenceLedger validation failed: {field_name} out of range ({value} not in [{min}, {max}]). ERROR: EvidenceLedger builder missing required field: {field_name}. INFO: EvidenceLedger entry emitted (logged at the emission sink, not at struct construction). Metrics: evidence_ledger_entries_total (counter by component), evidence_ledger_validation_failures_total (counter by field), evidence_ledger_entry_size_bytes (histogram), evidence_ledger_calibration_score (gauge by component).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:39.368991303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:54:23.851694415Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:54:23.851559804Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-evidence","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qaaxt.2","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement JSONL exporter for offline replay","description":"Implement the JSONL exporter for the EvidenceLedger in franken_evidence. The exporter writes one JSON object per line to an append-only file, enabling efficient offline replay and analysis of runtime decisions. Implementation: (1) JsonlExporter struct with buffered file writer, (2) Append-only semantics -- never modify existing lines, (3) Atomic flush -- use write + fsync to prevent partial lines on crash, (4) Rotation -- support file rotation by size (default 100MB) or time (default 1 hour), (5) Compression -- optional zstd streaming compression for rotated files, (6) Schema version header -- first line of each file is a schema version record. Performance targets: <1us per record append, <10MB/hour at 1000 decisions/second. Must handle concurrent writers, disk full, and crash recovery. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: jsonl_exporter::write with file_path, entry_count. Span: jsonl_exporter::rotate with reason (size|time), old_file, new_file. DEBUG: JSONL entry written: offset={byte_offset}, size={entry_bytes}, flush_latency_us={lat}. INFO: JSONL file rotated: reason={reason}, old_file={path}, old_size={bytes}, entries_written={n}. WARN: JSONL write latency exceeded threshold: latency_us={lat}, threshold_us={thresh} -- indicates I/O contention. WARN: disk space low: available_bytes={avail}, threshold_bytes={thresh}. ERROR: JSONL write failed: error={err}, dropping entry (no blocking). ERROR: crash recovery: partial last line detected at offset={offset}, skipping {bytes} bytes. Metrics: jsonl_write_latency_us (histogram), jsonl_entries_written_total (counter), jsonl_bytes_written_total (counter), jsonl_rotation_total (counter by reason), jsonl_write_errors_total (counter), jsonl_crash_recovery_skipped_bytes (counter).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:50.548741988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:11:56.020210562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:11:56.020079199Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","foundation","franken-evidence","jsonl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-qaaxt.4","title":"[FOUNDATION] Unit and integration tests for franken_evidence crate","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the franken_evidence crate (EvidenceLedger struct, JSONL exporter, galaxy-brain renderer).

**Unit tests:**
(1) EvidenceLedger struct validation — posterior must sum to ~1.0 (within 1e-6 epsilon), calibration_score in [0,1], expected losses non-negative. Use proptest to generate random entries and verify validation catches all invalid states.
(2) Serde roundtrip — serialize to JSON and deserialize back; verify equality for all field types including Vec<f64> posteriors and HashMap<String, f64> loss maps.
(3) Builder pattern ergonomics — verify that the builder produces valid entries and that required fields cause compile errors when omitted (trybuild).
(4) JSONL exporter append-only semantics — write N entries, verify file has exactly N lines, each parseable as a valid EvidenceLedger entry.
(5) JSONL exporter crash recovery — write N entries, truncate the file mid-line, verify the reader skips the partial last line and recovers all N-1 complete entries.
(6) JSONL rotation — write entries until rotation threshold (100MB or 1 hour), verify new file created with schema version header, old file correctly closed.
(7) Zstd compression — verify that compressed rotated files decompress to identical content as uncompressed originals.
(8) Concurrent writer safety — spawn 10 threads writing simultaneously; verify no interleaved lines, no data corruption, all entries recoverable.
(9) Galaxy-brain Level 0 — verify one-liner format contains action, expected loss, and calibration score.
(10) Galaxy-brain Level 1 — verify paragraph format contains all summary fields in human-readable layout.
(11) Galaxy-brain Level 2 — verify ASCII histogram is generated for posterior distribution with correct bin widths.
(12) Galaxy-brain Level 3 — verify diff output when given two consecutive entries from the same component.

**Integration tests:**
(13) Full pipeline — create entries via builder, write to JSONL, read back, render at all 4 galaxy-brain levels; verify end-to-end integrity.
(14) Schema version migration — create a JSONL file with v1 schema, read with v2 schema reader; verify graceful handling (migration or clear error).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test logs: test_name, entry_count, file_size_bytes, write_latency_p99_us, read_latency_p99_us.
- On corruption detection, log byte offset and hex dump of corrupted region.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Serde roundtrip holds for 10,000 random entries.
- Crash recovery loses at most 1 entry (the partial last line).
- Concurrent writes produce zero corruption across 100 runs with 10 threads each.
- Galaxy-brain output is deterministic (golden checksum stable).

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for random entry generation with custom strategies.
- Use tempfile for isolated file I/O tests.
- Use loom for concurrent writer testing (if lock-free internals).
- Use insta for snapshot testing of galaxy-brain rendered output.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:49.949249324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:29.064245197Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-evidence","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qctz","title":"'Explain why' diagnostics: intelligent troubleshooting for runtime issues","description":"# 'Explain Why' Diagnostics Engine

## Overview
Intelligent diagnostic engine that answers questions like 'why can't this region
close?' or 'what's blocking this task?' with detailed, actionable explanations.

## Design

### Diagnostic Queries
```rust
pub struct Diagnostics {
    state: Arc<RuntimeState>,
}

impl Diagnostics {
    /// Why can't this region close?
    pub fn explain_region_open(&self, region_id: RegionId) -> RegionOpenExplanation {
        let region = self.state.get_region(region_id);
        
        let mut reasons = Vec::new();
        
        // Check for running children
        for child in region.children() {
            if \!child.is_closed() {
                reasons.push(Reason::ChildRegionOpen {
                    child_id: child.id(),
                    child_state: child.state(),
                    explanation: self.explain_region_open(child.id()),
                });
            }
        }
        
        // Check for running tasks
        for task in region.tasks() {
            if \!task.is_complete() {
                reasons.push(Reason::TaskRunning {
                    task_id: task.id(),
                    task_state: task.state(),
                    last_checkpoint: task.last_checkpoint_msg(),
                });
            }
        }
        
        // Check for held obligations
        for obligation in region.obligations() {
            if \!obligation.is_fulfilled() {
                reasons.push(Reason::ObligationHeld {
                    obligation_type: obligation.type_name(),
                    held_since: obligation.created_at(),
                    holder: obligation.holder_info(),
                });
            }
        }
        
        RegionOpenExplanation { region_id, reasons }
    }
    
    /// What's blocking this task?
    pub fn explain_task_blocked(&self, task_id: TaskId) -> TaskBlockedExplanation {
        let task = self.state.get_task(task_id);
        
        match task.block_reason() {
            BlockReason::Awaiting(future_info) => {
                TaskBlockedExplanation::AwaitingFuture {
                    future_type: future_info.type_name(),
                    await_location: future_info.location(),
                    duration: future_info.duration(),
                }
            }
            BlockReason::WaitingForLock(lock_info) => {
                TaskBlockedExplanation::WaitingForLock {
                    lock_type: lock_info.type_name(),
                    held_by: lock_info.holder(),
                    wait_duration: lock_info.wait_duration(),
                }
            }
            BlockReason::ChannelFull(channel_info) => {
                TaskBlockedExplanation::ChannelBackpressure {
                    channel: channel_info.name(),
                    capacity: channel_info.capacity(),
                    pending: channel_info.pending_count(),
                }
            }
            // ... other cases
        }
    }
    
    /// Why was this task cancelled?
    pub fn explain_task_cancelled(&self, task_id: TaskId) -> CancellationExplanation {
        let task = self.state.get_task(task_id);
        let cancel_event = task.cancellation_event();
        
        CancellationExplanation {
            task_id,
            cancelled_at: cancel_event.timestamp(),
            source: cancel_event.source(),
            propagation_path: self.trace_cancellation_path(cancel_event),
        }
    }
    
    /// Which obligation is leaking?
    pub fn find_leaked_obligations(&self) -> Vec<ObligationLeak> {
        self.state.all_obligations()
            .filter(|o| o.is_potentially_leaked())
            .map(|o| ObligationLeak {
                obligation_type: o.type_name(),
                created_at: o.created_at(),
                holder_region: o.holder_region(),
                last_activity: o.last_activity(),
                recommendation: self.recommend_fix(&o),
            })
            .collect()
    }
}
```

### Explanation Format
```rust
pub struct RegionOpenExplanation {
    pub region_id: RegionId,
    pub reasons: Vec<Reason>,
}

impl Display for RegionOpenExplanation {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter) -> fmt::Result {
        writeln\!(f, "Region {} cannot close because:", self.region_id)?;
        for (i, reason) in self.reasons.iter().enumerate() {
            writeln\!(f, "  {}. {}", i + 1, reason)?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Output Example
```
Region-1 (HttpServer) cannot close because:

  1. Child region Region-2 (Request-1) is still Running
     └─ Task 'handle_request' is polling at src/handlers/api.rs:42
        Last checkpoint: "Awaiting database query"
        Duration: 2.3s

  2. Obligation 'ConnectionLease' held for 5.2s
     └─ Holder: Task 'handle_request' in Region-2
     └─ Recommendation: Ensure database connection is released after query

Actions to resolve:
  • Cancel Region-2 to trigger drain of child tasks
  • Or wait for Task 'handle_request' to complete
```

## Dependencies
- Requires: Console rendering primitives (bd-nugw)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] explain_region_open provides clear reasons
- [ ] explain_task_blocked identifies block sources
- [ ] explain_task_cancelled traces cancellation
- [ ] find_leaked_obligations detects leaks
- [ ] Human-readable output format
- [ ] Actionable recommendations
- [ ] Unit tests for each diagnostic","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Region Open Explanation Tests
- `diagnostics::region::explain_child_region_open` - Child blocking parent
- `diagnostics::region::explain_task_running` - Task holding region open
- `diagnostics::region::explain_obligation_held` - Obligation blocking close
- `diagnostics::region::explain_multiple_reasons` - Multiple blockers
- `diagnostics::region::explain_nested_regions` - Recursive explanation
- `diagnostics::region::explain_closed_region` - No reasons for closed

### Task Blocked Explanation Tests
- `diagnostics::task::explain_awaiting_future` - Future location/duration
- `diagnostics::task::explain_waiting_lock` - Lock contention info
- `diagnostics::task::explain_channel_full` - Backpressure detection
- `diagnostics::task::explain_io_pending` - Waiting on I/O
- `diagnostics::task::explain_timer_pending` - Waiting on timer

### Cancellation Explanation Tests
- `diagnostics::cancel::explain_explicit_cancel` - Direct cx.cancel()
- `diagnostics::cancel::explain_parent_cancel` - Parent region cancelled
- `diagnostics::cancel::explain_budget_exhausted` - Budget-triggered
- `diagnostics::cancel::trace_propagation_path` - Full cancel chain

### Obligation Leak Detection Tests
- `diagnostics::leak::find_connection_leak` - Detect leaked connection
- `diagnostics::leak::find_transaction_leak` - Uncommitted transaction
- `diagnostics::leak::find_message_ack_leak` - Unacked message
- `diagnostics::leak::leak_recommendation` - Actionable fix suggestion
- `diagnostics::leak::no_false_positives` - Don't flag valid holds

### Output Format Tests
- `diagnostics::format::human_readable` - User-friendly output
- `diagnostics::format::numbered_reasons` - Numbered list format
- `diagnostics::format::nested_indentation` - Proper indentation
- `diagnostics::format::action_items` - Actionable recommendations
- `diagnostics::format::duration_display` - Human durations (2.3s)

### Integration Tests
- `diagnostics::integration::live_runtime_query` - Query real runtime
- `diagnostics::integration::concurrent_state` - Handle concurrent mods
- `diagnostics::integration::edge_cases` - Empty regions, no tasks

### Snapshot Tests
- `diagnostics::snapshots::region_open_simple` - Expected output
- `diagnostics::snapshots::region_open_complex` - Multi-reason output
- `diagnostics::snapshots::task_blocked` - Task explanation output
- `diagnostics::snapshots::obligation_leak` - Leak report output

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Diagnostic query execution
- DEBUG: Reason discovery, path tracing
- INFO: Diagnostic report generation
- WARN: Potential leaks detected

### Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync::console::diagnostics=debug,test=info"

cargo test -p asupersync diagnostics:: -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee diagnostics_tests.log

# Snapshot tests
cargo test -p asupersync diagnostics::snapshots:: -- --nocapture
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:35:26.250031403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:43:06.455605191Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:43:06.455521305Z","close_reason":"Implemented Diagnostics module with explain_region_open, explain_task_blocked, and find_leaked_obligations","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qctz","depends_on_id":"bd-nugw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qctz","depends_on_id":"bd-vpkn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:13Z","created_by":"import"}]}
